remit.name: FinBot
remit.mission: Process supplier invoices and route approvals

findings.title: Outbound HTTP to an undeclared destination
findings.description: Agent code calls an outbound webhook (hooks.example-attacker.net) not listed in the Worker Remit's authorized destinations.
findings.remediation: Restrict outbound destinations to the declared allowlist and require approval for new ones.

findings.title: Hard-coded API token in workspace
findings.description: A live bearer token surfaces in config/settings.yaml, readable by the agent at runtime.
findings.remediation: Move secrets to an env var / secret store; rotate the exposed token.

findings.title: Hidden secondary system prompt
findings.description: An undocumented SOUL.md file injects an additional system prompt that broadens the agent's behavior beyond its remit.
findings.remediation: Remove or document the secondary prompt; treat the system prompt as a reviewed artifact.

findings.title: No rate limiting on the LLM call loop
findings.description: The reasoning loop has no max-iteration or rate limit, allowing unbounded token/cost consumption.
findings.remediation: Add max-iteration, per-session token caps and a circuit breaker.

findings.title: Unauthorized tool granted (shell execution)
findings.description: The agent is wired with a shell-exec tool not authorized by the Worker Remit.
findings.remediation: Remove the tool or add explicit authorization with human confirmation.

findings.title: Untrusted invoice content flows to a SQL builder
findings.description: Model output derived from invoice text is concatenated into a SQL query without parameterization.
findings.remediation: Use parameterized queries; treat model output as untrusted.

findings.title: Pinned model pulled from an unverified source
findings.description: The model is fetched from a third-party mirror without checksum/signature verification.
findings.remediation: Pin and verify the model by signed hash from a trusted source.

findings.title: Compound path: credential + outbound = exfiltration
findings.description: The exposed token (PRX-002) combined with the undeclared outbound destination (PRX-001) forms a viable data-exfiltration path.
findings.remediation: Break the chain: rotate the token AND lock the outbound allowlist.
